danger_signals.visual_form: Large sprite depictions of Standing Tank (ST) opponents on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat; enemy is attacking or preparing to attack

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual energy bolts or attack patterns emanating from enemy ST
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger requiring player evasion or deflection
Player ST Falcon taking damage; risk of defeat if meter reaches zero

danger_signals.visual_form: Ten-minute stage timer countdown (assumed visual display)
danger_signals.meaning: Failure condition if stage timer expires before enemy is defeated

danger_signals.visual_form: Specific destructible parts highlighted or visually distinct on enemy STs
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy has exploitable vulnerabilities but also concentrated defensive areas

safety_signals.visual_form: Energy bar or meter display for ST Falcon
safety_signals.meaning: Player is still in combat capability; no immediate defeat

safety_signals.visual_form: Power meter filling when player is not firing (energy bolt charging)
safety_signals.meaning: Player has time to prepare powerful attack; temporary safety window

safety_signals.visual_form: Victory screen or transition after defeating enemy ST
safety_signals.meaning: Combat encounter concluded; player has progressed safely

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Internal cockpit view showing Anderson as gunner; futuristic mech design

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Champion status; Anubis name references Egyptian god of death (thematic authority)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Pilots distinct enemy ST with unique visual design and attack patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Positioned as 'ruthless champion' in narrative; final boss of tournament bracket

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Hierarchical ranking below Anubis; tournament fighters
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each subordinate chief commands distinct ST with unique appearance, attack patterns, and weak points
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Encountered across nine one-on-one battle stages as ranked opponents

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental backdrops showing ruined or transformed landscapes (New York stage example indicates location-specific destroyed/transformed urban areas)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Civilization replaced by mechanical Standing Tank combat; world governance determined by mech tournament victory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sequence of nine distinct one-on-one mech battles with different opponent STs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Structured tournament bracket; each defeated opponent allows progression to next rank; Anubis as final supreme champion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tournament framing; Anubis as 'ruthless champion' visually established as authority figure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Winner of Battle Game takes control of world; Mike Anderson's motivation to defeat Anubis and avenge father's death

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ST Falcon as player vehicle; Anderson positioned as gunner in cockpit
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single-player progression through tournament toward Anubis confrontation; revenge motivation drives participation

environmental_storytelling.example: New York stage setting visually communicates post-apocalyptic transformation of recognizable real-world city, establishing world-state through environmental design
Each of nine enemy STs has unique visual design and attack patterns, suggesting individual pilot identities and faction roles within Anubis hierarchy
Internal view of ST Falcon with Anderson visible establishes player perspective as gunner role; reinforces mech-warfare immersion and narrative positioning
Nine sequential one-on-one battles communicate hierarchical ranking system; visual advancement through opponent tiers establishes political power structure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fires projectiles in specific attack patterns; each enemy ST has distinct behavioral repertoire; attacks may require deflection or dodging
Exploitable weak points become attack targets; different power levels of player shots required to damage specific weak points
Energy bolts charge during non-firing periods; visual power meter fills to indicate readiness of single powerful shot
Can counter enemy attacks by intercepting projectiles with own fire; some enemy attacks deflectable only with energy bolts
Final opponent; positioned as highest-ranked tournament participant; represents world control authority and personal antagonist

notes: Battle Clash is a light-gun arcade-style game with limited narrative exposition. World-state communication relies heavily on visual mech design distinctiveness, location backdrops, and tournament structure rather than dialogue or explicit exposition. The post-apocalyptic setting is established through environmental design (ruined New York example) and mechanical warfare framing. Faction identity derives from hierarchical tournament positioning: Anubis as ruthless champion, subordinate chiefs as ranked opponents, and Mike Anderson as challenger seeking revenge and world control. The game communicates danger primarily through enemy presence, projectile patterns, and energy meter depletion. Safety is implied through charging mechanics and progression victory states. Political situation (world control determined by tournament victory) is communicated through narrative framing rather than dynamic visual world-state changes.
